title: Exhaustive upstream test-file inventory (evidence-backed accounting)
purpose: Every conformance-relevant upstream test file individually classified, with audited totals, so 'all existing tests we could port' is evidence-backed rather than a selection. Verified by verify.ts against the mono checkout at auditedUpstreamSha. Companion to ledger.json.

portabilityVocabulary.differential: Drives a query and compares results against a fresh Postgres oracle (or pins a specific query result). Conceptually portable to a black-box harness that speaks the wire and diffs vs an oracle/reference.
portabilityVocabulary.in-process: Drives the mono module graph directly (IVM source changes, planner internals, scalar pre-resolve, plan flags, in-memory unit tests, legacy encodings, CPU profiling). Not portable to a different server implementation.

zqlIntegrationTests.aggregateNote: 'portability' is whether the TEST can leave mono's IVM. 'orezStatus' is whether Orez re-expresses the BEHAVIOR black-box. They are independent: an in-process test can still be equivalent/partial if Orez covers the behavior over the wire (e.g. fuzz.test.ts).

files.purpose: capability/edge cases; boots initialSync PG->SQLite then applies IVM source changes (add/edit/remove)
files.reason: drives IVM makeSourceChange* directly; no wire surface.

files.purpose: child-relationship merge correctness after initialSync
pins one ZQL string (nested whereExists + limit(0)) vs PG
L0/L1 fuzzer backbone (bounded-exhaustive + pairwise + push + flip + yield) vs PG, asserts 100% pairwise coverage
files.gap: MISSING: pairwise coverage gate AND bounded-exhaustive enumeration (orez samples randomly). See ledger fuzzModules coverage.ts/cover.ts/skeleton.ts.

files.purpose: seeded randomized fuzz cases + auto-shrink, differential vs PG
files.gap: MISSING: auto-shrink (orez records seed+FULL spec, unminimized). See ledger fuzzModules shrink.ts.

files.purpose: replay committed minimized repros (chinook/regressions/*.json)
files.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts writes artifacts to harness/regressions/ but does not replay them
files.gap: missing committed replay-first corpus gate (ledger rankedGaps #2).

files.purpose: nightly scale lane: full chinook, thousands of rows
files.gap: MISSING: a thousands-of-rows DIFFERENTIAL. Orez measures scaling curves (clients x queries x write-rate, rss/lag) at scale but does not run the correctness differential over a large dataset.

files.purpose: scalar-subquery differential lane
files.reason: depends on zqlite resolve-scalar-subqueries; only meaningful vs in-process IVM's non-native scalar handling.

files.purpose: join-flip plan equivalence vs PG
files.reason: flip is a mono IVM plan flag with no wire/pg analog.

files.purpose: L1 replica + L2 protocol-materialized differential vs a fresh PG oracle (richest oracle)
files.note: upstream connects to view-syncer in-process (bypasses the socket); orez runs the cross-implementation differential over the real wire.

files.purpose: hand-written chinook differential vs PG; pins brokenRelationshipLimits (junction limit/orderBy gaps)
files.note: junction limit/orderBy gap encoded as ledger knownXfails; orez fixture has no junction relationship yet.

files.purpose: fast no-PG generator unit tests (enumeration counts, covering-array completeness, metamorphic/flip self-consistency)
files.gap: MISSING: enumeration-count and covering-array-completeness assertions. Orez has metamorphic self-consistency (mutation-proven) + axis-count sanity only. Example of an in-process TEST with real black-box behavioral coverage.

files.purpose: query-planner plan-attempt validation (controlled)
files.reason: mono cost-based IVM planner internals; orez has no such planner.

files.purpose: query-planner execution validation vs PG
query-planner cost-model validation
collation: zql/zqlite byte-order vs PG locale-aware ordering (same set, relaxed order)
bigint (int8) edge through z2s/zqlite/PG
compiler with query args across types
files.orezArtifact: harness/src/sweep.ts (passes typed args) but does not pin all type edges
files.gap: partial: args are exercised, per-type edge assertions are not pinned.

files.purpose: core z2s compiler differential (json metadata, nullable FK, timestamps)
files.note: the z2s ZQL->SQL compiler itself has no orez analog (orez ships snapshots / compiles in the rust core); the value round-trip it protects is covered by shapes.

files.purpose: regression repro from a Discord bug report (in-process edit source change)
files.reason: in-process IVM repro; note as a candidate for the orez repro corpus once black-box shrink lands.

files.purpose: planner plan-attempt validation on the pagila graph
CPU profile (.cpuprofile) of a normalized-OR + flipped-exists query at 8x topology
files.reason: a performance profiler, not a conformance test.

files.purpose: text semantics: LIKE/ILIKE/NULL logic; pins the ILIKE Unicode-folding known divergence under COLLATE C vs default
files.orezArtifact: harness/src/fixture.ts uses LIKE/ILIKE patterns but does not pin the COLLATE-C divergence

files.purpose: reading legacy v20 array-style column encodings from zqlite
files.reason: legacy encoding read; orez targets the current encoding.

zeroCache.note: Server-internals: replicator, change-streamer, change-source (pg), view-syncer, mutagen/pusher, auth, db, types, config, workers. As TESTS, all but one are in-process (they drive mono's own server module graph, not a wire). That is a statement about test PORTABILITY, not about behavioral coverage: several categories validate BEHAVIORS Orez re-expresses black-box in its own lanes. The two are tracked separately below. Exactly ONE test is a genuine over-the-wire v51 websocket test.
zeroCache.testPortabilityNote: Only integration/integration.pg.test.ts drives a real websocket. The other 142 drive mono modules directly and cannot run against a different server implementation as-is.

categories.note: Validates the v51 downstream/poke contract + hydrate/advance + TTL + permissions + CVR. Orez exercises the downstream/poke contract, hydrate, and incremental maintenance black-box; TTL, CVR internals, and view-syncer permissions internals are not covered.

logical-replication internals (initial-sync, backfill, toasted values, slot health). Relevant to Orez's pg-proxy/replication planes, where Orez has its own recovery/restore/startup integration coverage; the sync-server core the conformance harness targets ships snapshots and does not replicate.
incremental-sync / resumption — Orez has recovery/restore lanes for its own replication path.
dispatcher/inspector/otel; some behaviors observable black-box, most internal.

read/write-authorizer permission-filter logic. Orez covers pull visibility + membership-driven revocation black-box; push-role authorization, auth-token refresh/staleness, and external-ACL invalidation are greenfield (matches upstream L4-future).
pusher / custom-mutator application. Orez exercises v51 custom-mutator push + LMID + app-error rollback black-box; the pusher/combinePushes internals are not.
server-internal change-stream wire format, not the client sync protocol.
integration.pg.test.ts — the only real ws v51 test; Orez drives real stock clients over the wire against every target and adds convergence + oracle equality.
unit/integration files across config/workers/remaining services not individually broken out; internal.

behavioralCoverageSummary.note: By CATEGORY, how much of the behavior Orez re-expresses black-box. This is NOT a per-file status; it corrects the earlier overclaim that 142 files have no analog.

zeroProtocol.note: valita round-trip tests that DEFINE the v51 wire contract (ast, client-schema, push, connect, protocol-version, error, analyze-query-result, and type-tests). orezStatus: upstream-baseline (the spec orez conforms to, not something to port).

fuzzModules.note: The coverage-driven fuzzer. Each of the 20 files is individually classified in ledger.json.fuzzModules (19 library modules + fuzz.test.ts).

verification.run: bun harness/upstream-parity/verify.ts   (from repo root, with ~/github/mono present)
contract: the mono checkout resolves origin/main and its SHA is recorded/compared to auditedUpstreamSha (drift => warning, non-zero unless --allow-drift)
the set of packages/zql-integration-tests/src/**/*.test.ts at the audited SHA EQUALS the 24 paths listed here (no missing, no extra)
zero-cache test-file count == 143, zero-protocol == 9, fuzz non-test modules == 19
on any mismatch the audit is stale: verify.ts exits non-zero and prints the delta
